H-OS-L1.name: Pebble
H-OS-L1.sphere: Pebble carries strong confidence (n=95, mean=7.15, std=1.78, pct_pos=66%) — highest n in library. Distribution shows clear rightward skew with modes at 9 (26) and 10 (17). Qualitative (n=29): 'Noticeable improvement' modal (12), 10-min settle (11), positive music ('Liked it' 10, 'Loved it!' 9). YouTube reach is highest at 30 views but retention data is NULL. AutoGluon predicts 6.859, lower than observed — model may undervalue due to NULL retention features.
H-OS-L1.sphere_plain: Pebble is the most popular protocol in the library with the best outcomes for over-stimulation relief — listeners love the experience and recommend it for overwhelming moments.

H-OS-L2.name: Rainfall
H-OS-L2.sphere: Rainfall shows strong confidence (n=51, mean=6.92, std=1.73, pct_pos=65%). Expected modest drop from L1 (~0.23 points) with distribution at 7-9. Qualitative (n=21) shows 'Noticeable improvement' modal and 10-min settle, mirroring L1. Music opinion more divided than L1 ('Neutral' 6, no negatives in L1). AutoGluon predicts 6.859, closely aligning. Drift at sim=0.780 shows relative stability.
H-OS-L2.sphere_plain: Rainfall works well too, though slightly less than Pebble — it takes about 10 minutes to settle, and some listeners have neutral feelings about the music.

H-OS-L3.name: Glacier
H-OS-L3.sphere: Glacier carries meaningful confidence (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Breaks monotonic pattern by scoring above L2 — creating U-shaped efficacy. Distribution peaks at 8 (11) and 9 (10). Qualitative (n=23) shows faster settle than L2 (5 min for 7) and substantial sleep use (8 of 23), suggesting L3 may induce faster parasympathetic shift. Flagged as most analytically distinct (mean_sim=0.677), with sphere treating it differently due to inverted pattern.
H-OS-L3.sphere_plain: Glacier is interesting because it's actually faster and more effective than Rainfall despite being the strongest intensity — paradoxically, some listeners find the deeper level more calming.

H-AX-L1.name: Meadow
H-AX-L1.sphere: Meadow demonstrates strong confidence (n=67, mean=7.22, std=1.93, pct_pos=63%). The distribution shows a rightward skew with mode at 8-9, consistent with effective anxiety descent. Qualitative data (n=23) shows 'Moderate Improvement' as modal change rating with settle time at ~10 minutes; music opinion skews positive ('Liked it' 10, 'Loved it!' 6). YouTube reach is minimal (7 views) with no retention data. AutoGluon predicts 7.838 for anxiety zone — approximately 0.6 points above observed — suggesting latent signal the current sample hasn't fully captured.
H-AX-L1.sphere_plain: Meadow is the most responded-to protocol in the library, with listeners reporting good anxiety relief and a pleasant listening experience — though it hasn't reached many people on YouTube yet.

H-AX-L2.name: Cottage
H-AX-L2.sphere: Cottage shows meaningful confidence (n=48, mean=6.94, std=1.62, pct_pos=67%). Distribution tightly clusters at 7-8 with mode at 8 (15 of 48), showing consistent listener response. Qualitative (n=20) reveals bimodal settle pattern (5 min and 20 min) suggesting two listener populations. Music opinion is more divided than L1 with 5 'Neutral' responses, indicating the VOL001 groove style at L2 may be less universally appealing. AutoGluon prediction (7.838) exceeds observed by ~0.9 points.
H-AX-L2.sphere_plain: Cottage performs well for anxiety but shows more mixed reactions than Meadow — some listeners settled quickly, others took longer, suggesting it works differently depending on the person.

H-AX-L3.name: Valley
H-AX-L3.sphere: Valley maintains meaningful confidence (n=36, mean=7.08, std=1.89, pct_pos=61%). The distribution is flatter than L1/L2, reversing the expected monotonic descent pattern — scores across 6-10 rather than clustering at the upper end. Qualitative (n=20) shows longest settle time in the subcategory (10 min for 10 respondents) and 'Very settling/calming' rhythm (10 of 20). YouTube reach is negligible (4 views). Panel divided on whether this reflects genuine L3 amplification or sampling noise.
H-AX-L3.sphere_plain: Valley shows strong results but takes longer to work than the other anxiety protocols — listeners who stick with it report good outcomes, but it requires more patience.

H-CR-L1.name: Anchor
H-CR-L1.sphere: Anchor performs at meaningful confidence (n=43, mean=6.35, std=1.70, pct_pos=53%) — as structurally expected for crisis protocols where listeners arrive in acute distress. Distribution shows mode at 9 (12 of 43), indicating strong relief for a subgroup. Qualitative (n=24) is notably positive: 'Significant improvement' modal (8 of 24) with fast settle (~5 min for 8). AutoGluon predicts crisis-zone score of 7.955 but routes to H-CR-L2, potentially signalling stronger signal in the L2 variant.
H-CR-L1.sphere_plain: Anchor is designed for crisis moments, so the scores are lower than other HEALING protocols — that's expected since people using it are in acute distress. But those who use it often report significant relief.

H-CR-L2.name: Harbor
H-CR-L2.sphere: Harbor shows meaningful confidence (n=33, mean=6.52, std=1.70, pct_pos=55%), slightly outperforming L1. Distribution concentrates at 8-9 (15 of 33) with thin left tail. Qualitative (n=16) reveals division: 'Neutral' as modal music opinion (6 of 16) while 'Loved it!' scores high (5 of 16). Settle time clusters at 10 minutes. YouTube views=4. Cross-run drift (sim=0.662) is notable.
H-CR-L2.sphere_plain: Harbor performs slightly better than Anchor for crisis moments, with many listeners finding it very settling — though there's a split in how people feel about the music.

H-CR-L3.name: Horizon
H-CR-L3.sphere: Horizon maintains meaningful confidence (n=27, mean=6.52, std=1.91, pct_pos=52%) — tied with L2 but higher variance. Distribution peaks at 8 but stretches across full 3-10 range. Qualitative (n=19) shows uniquely fast settle: 7 of 19 settled immediately, the fastest in crisis subcategory. However, 4 of 19 gave 'Not liked' music opinion — the only HEALING protocol with explicit dislikes in qualitative data, flagged by two analyzers as warranting investigation into L3 aversiveness.
H-CR-L3.sphere_plain: Horizon works fastest for crisis — many people settle within the first minute — but it also has the most mixed reviews in the crisis category, with some not liking the music.

H-SL-L1.name: Lantern
H-SL-L1.sphere: Lantern holds strong confidence (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, pct_pos=65%). Distribution shows clear right tail — 5 scored 10, 11 scored 9. Qualitative (n=15): 'Noticeable' (6) and 'Moderate' (5) improvements dominate; settle split between 5 min (5) and immediate (3). AutoGlouon predicts sleep-zone 7.539 and selects H-SL-L1 as zone-optimal. Notable cross-run drift (sim=0.530) — second-highest in library — indicates framing has shifted substantially with growing n.
H-SL-L1.sphere_plain: Lantern is the strongest sleep protocol with a solid track record — it helps people settle and creates good improvement reported across multiple listeners.

H-SL-L2.name: Hammock
H-SL-L2.sphere: Hammock shows meaningful confidence (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, pct_pos=67%). Distribution tightly clustered at 7-8 (24 of 45), lowest std in sleep subcategory — most consistent listener experience. Qualitative (n=15) reveals unusual listener mix: 'A client/patient' (6) and 'Someone I support' (5) outnumber 'Myself' (4), suggesting high caregiver/clinician deployment. Longest settle in subcategory (10 min for 7) with 3 'Too busy' rhythm opinions. Semantic clustering tightly links to H-SL-L1 (sim=0.869).
H-SL-L2.sphere_plain: Hammock performs similarly to Lantern with very consistent results — it's often used by caregivers or clinicians with their clients rather than for personal use.

H-SL-L3.name: Midnight
H-SL-L3.sphere: Midnight holds meaningful confidence (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Nearly identical to L2 in mean and distribution shape. Qualitative (n=22) richest for sleep: 'Noticeable' (7), 'Moderate' (5), 'Significant' (5) well-represented. Fastest settle in subcategory (5 min for 8), but 7 of 22 rated rhythm 'Slightly busy' — highest in sleep. Cross-run drift at 0.611 moderate but notable. AutoGluon does not specifically flag; selects H-SL-L1 as zone lead.
H-SL-L3.sphere_plain: Midnight shows good sleep support with varied improvement levels — it's the fastest to work but some listeners find the rhythm slightly busy.

T-FC-L1.name: Compass
T-FC-L1.sphere: Compass shows meaningful confidence (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, pct_pos=56%) — lowest THRIVING protocol. Distribution spreads wide with mode at 6-7 but high-score tail (10 respondents at 9-10). Qualitative (n=23): 'Noticeable improvement' dominant; rapid settle for many; rhythm 'Slightly busy' (9 of 23) — flagged by two analyzers as potential issue for a Focus HOLD protocol. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus zone but routes to L3, suggesting L1 underperforms zone potential.
T-FC-L1.sphere_plain: Compass is the weakest THRIVING protocol — some listeners get good results but overall it's underperforming, with many finding the rhythm slightly too busy for a focus protocol.

T-FC-L2.name: Waypoint
T-FC-L2.sphere: Waypoint has meaningful confidence (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, pct_pos=66%). Distribution tighter with clear mode at 7-8. Qualitative (n=17): 'Moderate' (5) and 'Significant' (5) gains; settle at 10 min dominant; balanced rhythm. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon prediction (7.73) modestly higher than observed; drift at 0.633 shows moderate stability.
T-FC-L2.sphere_plain: Waypoint shows solid focus improvement — most listeners settle around 10 minutes and report meaningful gains in clarity.

T-FC-L3.name: Summit
T-FC-L3.sphere: Summit carries meaningful confidence (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, pct_pos=71%) — highest-scoring protocol in entire library. Distribution is bimodal: cluster at 6-7 and another at 9-10 (10 respondents). Qualitative (n=29): highest 'Significant improvement' count (11); settle at 10 min for most; rhythm balanced or slightly busy. YouTube views=15 — largest reach in THRIVING. AutoGluon focus score 7.73 aligns well; cluster analysis shows analytical distinction (mean_sim=0.692).
T-FC-L3.sphere_plain: Summit is the highest-performing protocol in the entire library — strong improvement reports and the best YouTube reach in THRIVING, suggesting it genuinely works.

T-CF-L1.name: Ember
T-CF-L1.sphere: Ember shows meaningful confidence (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, pct_pos=53%). Distribution peaks at 7-8 with noticeable left tail (9 scores at 6 or below). Qualitative (n=21): 'Slight improvement' modal (8), bimodal settle (immediate for 6, 10 min for 6), indicating two response populations. Positive music ('Liked it' 9, 'Loved it!' 6). Cross-run drift at 0.730, relatively stable. AutoGluon does not specifically flag.
T-CF-L1.sphere_plain: Ember works for creative flow but the effect is subtle for many people — some feel it immediately, others need 10 minutes to settle into the experience.

T-CF-L2.name: Current
T-CF-L2.sphere: Current demonstrates meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, pct_pos=58%). Nearly identical to L1 in outcome but tighter std (1.41 vs 1.73), suggesting more consistent experience. Qualitative (n=15): 'Significant improvement' modal (5), inverting L1's 'Slight' pattern — L2 intensity pushes HOLD effect from subtle to pronounced. Music opinion notably strong ('Loved it!' 6, 'Liked it' 6) with no negatives. Settle clusters at 10 min.
T-CF-L2.sphere_plain: Current pushes past the subtle effect of Ember — more listeners report significant improvements, with strong positive feelings about the music.

T-CF-L3.name: Aurora
T-CF-L3.sphere: Aurora shows developing confidence (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, pct_pos=67%) with lowest variance in Creative Flow. Distribution peaks at 7-8 with minimal tail. Qualitative (n=25) reports 'Noticeable' (7) and 'Significant' (6) improvements; settle at 5-10 min for most. Music opinion divided ('Liked it' 9, 'Neutral' 7) — potentially higher n needed. YouTube views=7. Drift at 0.602 suggests recent analytical shift.
T-CF-L3.sphere_plain: Aurora has the best creative flow results so far with positive ratings despite lower counts — seems to work well but needs more data to be sure.

T-MB-L1.name: Stone
T-MB-L1.sphere: Stone shows meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, pct_pos=64%). Distribution centered at 7-8 with low tail. Qualitative (n=23): 'Moderate' (6), 'Noticeable' (5), 'Significant' (5) — balanced improvement; settle often 20 min; rhythm balanced. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts maintenance-zone 7.69 (higher than observed). Drift at 0.578 notably high, indicating evolving interpretation.
T-MB-L1.sphere_plain: Stone provides good maintenance and balance support — most listeners need about 20 minutes to fully settle but report positive outcomes.

T-MB-L2.name: Axis
T-MB-L2.sphere: Axis shows meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, pct_pos=59%) — highest variance in THRIVING. Distribution wide with secondary peak at 9-10. Qualitative (n=16): 'Noticeable' dominant; settle at 5-10 min; rhythm balanced but many 'Slightly busy' (7). YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.69; observed substantially lower. Flagged by two analyzers as potential inconsistency in HOLD arc at L2 intensity.
T-MB-L2.sphere_plain: Axis has inconsistent results with high variance — some love it, others don't feel much effect, suggesting it might work best for specific listener types.

T-MB-L3.name: Orbit
T-MB-L3.sphere: Orbit has developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, pct_pos=71%). Distribution modestly left-skewed. Qualitative (n=19): 'Noticeable' improvements; settle at 5 min for most; rhythm balanced. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, modest gap from observed. Drift at 0.554 shows notable analytical shift.
T-MB-L3.sphere_plain: Orbit shows good maintenance support with fast action — works quickly but has limited data so far.

X-ME-L1.name: Breeze
X-ME-L1.sphere: Breeze shows meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, pct_pos=54%). Distribution spreads across 4-10. Qualitative (n=22): 'Moderate' (7), 'Significant' (6); settle at 20 min; music 'Loved it!' (10), 'Neutral' (6). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts X-ME-L1 as cross-bucket destination for nearly every zone — flagged as potential over-indexing on latent feature.
X-ME-L1.sphere_plain: Breeze shows moderate mood improvement — more people need 20 minutes to settle, but those who stick with it report good results.

X-ME-L2.name: Sunrise
X-ME-L2.sphere: Sunrise carries meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, pct_pos=64%). Distribution more right-skewed than L1. Qualitative (n=15): 'Significant' (7), 'Moderate' (4); settle split 20 min and immediate; music positive ('Liked it' 7, 'Loved it!' 6). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon cross-bucket routing includes this as destination for multiple zones.
X-ME-L2.sphere_plain: Sunrise performs similarly to Breeze with strong significant improvement reports — listeners enjoy the music and experience positive effects.

X-ME-L3.name: Updraft
X-ME-L3.sphere: Updraft shows developing confidence (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, pct_pos=56%) — inverts expected ASCENT progression (lower than L1 and L2). Distribution peaks at 7-8. Qualitative (n=25): 'Noticeable' (9), 'No effect' (5) — highest 'No effect' in series; settle at 5 min for most. Music opinion positive. YouTube views=4. Two analyzers flagged inverted level progression as structurally wrong for ASCENT — L3 may overshoot optimal arousal window.
X-ME-L3.sphere_plain: Updraft paradoxically scores lower than its predecessors despite being strongest — appears to overshoot the sweet spot for mood and energy.

X-MD-L1.name: Footpath
X-MD-L1.sphere: Footpath shows meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, pct_pos=45%) — lowest in Motivation subcategory. Distribution peaks at 5-6. Qualitative (n=17): 'Noticeable' (6), 'Significant' (5); settle at 5 min; music 'Liked it' (8) and 'Neutral' (5). YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts motivation-zone 7.876 but routes to X-MD-L1 as cross-bucket destination.
X-MD-L1.sphere_plain: Footpath has the lowest motivation scores with wide variance — doesn't seem to consistently work, with some experiencing improvement but others not.

X-MD-L2.name: Trail
X-MD-L2.sphere: Trail carries developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, pct_pos=43%) with 20-min modal settle time. Distribution moderately centered. Qualitative (n=21): 'Noticeable' (6), 'Moderate' (5); 'Slightly busy' as second-common rhythm opinion (6). YouTube views=7. Flagged by one analyzer as potentially too gradual ASCENT curve requiring steeper phi-sigmoid slope.
X-MD-L2.sphere_plain: Trail takes the longest to work in the motivation category — 20 minutes to settle suggests the effect may be too gradual for quick needs.

X-MD-L3.name: Switchback
X-MD-L3.sphere: Switchback has developing confidence (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, pct_pos=62%) — highest in Motivation subcategory despite low n. Distribution shows high-score tail. Qualitative (n=17): 'Moderate' (8), 'Noticeable' (5); fast settle (5 min); music positive. YouTube views=7. Highest cross-run drift (sim=0.507) indicates substantially shifting interpretation across runs.
X-MD-L3.sphere_plain: Switchback actually performs best for motivation despite low sample numbers — suggests higher intensity may be needed to drive motivation.

X-MC-L1.name: Cavern
X-MC-L1.sphere: Cavern shows meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, pct_pos=54%). Distribution modestly centered with slight tail to 8-9. Qualitative (n=25): 'Noticeable' (10) and 'Moderate' (8); settle ~10 min; rhythm balanced or very settling. Music opinion strong ('Loved it!' 9, 'Liked it' 9). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts mind-clarity 7.54 (higher than observed); cross-bucket links to X-ME-L1/L2.
X-MC-L1.sphere_plain: Cavern offers clear mind benefits with good music reception — listeners like it and report noticing improvements.

X-MC-L2.name: Starlight
X-MC-L2.sphere: Starlight has developing confidence (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, pct_pos=56%). Distribution symmetric similar to L1. YouTube views=12 — highest in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative (n=20): 'Significant' (7), 'Moderate' (6); settle split between immediate and 10 min; rhythm 'Very settling' (12). AutoGluon predicts 7.54; cross-bucket pattern consistent with overlap with X-ME protocols.
X-MC-L2.sphere_plain: Starlight is actually the most-watched TRANSFORMING video — strong 'very settling' feedback and significant improvement reports despite moderate scores.

X-MC-L3.name: Cosmos
X-MC-L3.sphere: Cosmos has developing confidence (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, pct_pos=50%) — lowest mean, highest variance in library. Distribution shows scores at 3, 7, 9 suggesting possible population split. Qualitative (n=17): 'Noticeable' (9) dominant but more 'No effect' (5) than other X-MC. YouTube views=8. Two analyzers flagged this as candidate population mismatch requiring subgroup analysis before increasing n. AutoGluon prediction unavailable due to low n.
X-MC-L3.sphere_plain: Cosmos has low scores and very mixed results — few people have tried it and the responses are all over the place, making it hard to know if it works.

sphere_commentary.overview:
The full dataset reveals a tight clustering of outcomes with TRANSFORMING slightly pulling the mean down while showing wider variance, consistent with ascending-arc design intent.
• HEALING (n=574, mean=6.98, std=1.77) and THRIVING (n=245, mean=6.96, std=1.72) are statistically indistinguishable at the series level — both showing strong signal.
• TRANSFORMING (n=181, mean=6.59, std=1.87) trails by roughly 0.4 points with higher spread — structurally expected for ascending from low-affect states.
• AutoGluon's top feature (avg_view_percentage, SHAP=0.277) is entirely NULL across all protocols, meaning the model's strongest predictor is currently uninformative and predictions should be treated as provisional.
• YouTube reach is uniformly low (views < 30 for all protocols), indicating the entire library remains undiscovered by the YouTube audience.
sphere_commentary.cross_series:
The three series show distinct efficacy profiles that reflect their design intent, with HEALING and THRIVING running neck-and-neck while TRANSFORMING shoulders expected difficulty of ascending-arc protocols.
• HEALING protocols show the tightest cluster with lowest variance, and crisis protocols (H-CR-*, mean≈6.4) perform as structurally expected — listeners arrive in acute distress.
• THRIVING shows the widest intra-series spread — T-FC-L3 (mean=7.63) is the highest-scoring protocol in the library while T-FC-L1 (mean=6.46) sits near the bottom, suggesting strong level-3 amplification.
• TRANSFORMING shows bimodality in Motivation & Drive — Footpath (L1, mean=6.36) and Trail (L2, mean=6.21) underperform while Switchback (L3, mean=7.15) jumps, consistent with ascending-arc protocols needing higher intensity.
sphere_commentary.anomalies:
Several protocol-level patterns diverge from expected monotonic level progression and warrant investigation.
• T-FC-L1 (Compass, n=41, mean=6.46) is the lowest-scoring THRIVING protocol despite being a HOLD at L1 — underperforms L2 (7.19) and L3 (7.63) by nearly a full point.
• X-MC-L3 (Cosmos, n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45) shows the highest variance in the library with the lowest mean — n=10 is too thin to determine signal vs. noise.
• X-ME-L3 (Updraft, n=18, mean=6.28) scores lower than L1 (Breeze, 6.74) and L2 (Sunrise, 6.76), inverting the expected ASCENT progression — this pattern appears in multiple analyzer runs.
• AutoGluon cross-bucket routing is notably repetitive: X-ME-L1 appears as cross-bucket destination for nearly every zone, suggesting potential over-indexing on a latent feature.
sphere_commentary.development_signals:
Several actionable signals emerge from convergent panel analysis that could improve protocol routing and design.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] T-FC-L1: Two analyzers flagged the 'Slightly busy' rhythm rating (9 of 23 respondents) — consider reducing rhythmic density at L1 to improve initial entrainment before the HOLD phase.
• [CONVERGENT SIGNAL] X-MC-L3: Two analyzers flagged the bimodal distribution and high variance suggesting a population split — flag for subgroup analysis before expanding n.
• T-FC-L3 (Summit, n=35, mean=7.63): Highest-scoring protocol in the library with 11 'Significant improvement' reports — consider promoting as flagship and prototyping an L4 variant to test ceiling effects.
• X-ME-L3: The inverted level progression pattern suggests L3 intensity may overshoot the optimal arousal window for Mood & Energy ASCENT — prototype an L3-variant with gentler delta.
• X-MD-L2 (Trail, n=14): Modal settle time at 20 minutes suggests the ASCENT curve may be too gradual — experiment with steeper phi-sigmoid slope.
sphere_commentary.embedding_analysis:
The embedding data reveals widespread analytical drift and semantic clustering patterns that reflect evolving data interpretation.
• CROSS-RUN DRIFT: Every protocol shows cosine similarity below 0.85, with several below 0.60 (X-MD-L3 at 0.507, H-SL-L1 at 0.530, T-FC-L3 at 0.532) — this indicates Sphere's commentary has shifted substantially, likely driven by growing n.
• CROSS-PROTOCOL SEMANTIC CLUSTERS: Tightest clusters are within-subcategory (T-FC-L1 ↔ T-FC-L2 at 0.878, H-SL-L1 ↔ H-SL-L2 at 0.869), confirming consistent grouping by design intent.
• Most analytically distinct protocols (H-OS-L3, T-FC-L3, T-CF-L3) are all L3 variants that may pull away due to stronger efficacy signals.
• Confidence note: Drift data is based on only two runs for most protocols — three or more runs would provide a meaningful trend line.

Qualitative (n=21) shows 'Noticeable improvement' modal and 10-min settle, mirroring L1. Music opinion more divided than L1 ('Neutral' 6, no negatives in L1). AutoGluon predicts 6.859, closely aligning. Drift at sim=0.780 shows relative stability.
by_protocol.H-OS-L3: Glacier carries meaningful confidence (n=47, mean=7.04, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Breaks monotonic pattern by scoring above L2 — creating U-shaped efficacy. Distribution peaks at 8 (11) and 9 (10). Qualitative (n=23) shows faster settle than L2 (5 min for 7) and substantial sleep use (8 of 23), suggesting L3 may induce faster parasympathetic shift. Flagged as most analytically distinct (mean_sim=0.677), with sphere treating it differently due to inverted pattern.
by_protocol.H-SL-L1: Lantern holds strong confidence (n=54, mean=7.19, std=1.86, pct_pos=65%). Distribution shows clear right tail — 5 scored 10, 11 scored 9. Qualitative (n=15): 'Noticeable' (6) and 'Moderate' (5) improvements dominate; settle split between 5 min (5) and immediate (3). AutoGlouon predicts sleep-zone 7.539 and selects H-SL-L1 as zone-optimal. Notable cross-run drift (sim=0.530) — second-highest in library — indicates framing has shifted substantially with growing n.
by_protocol.H-SL-L2: Hammock shows meaningful confidence (n=45, mean=7.09, std=1.55, pct_pos=67%). Distribution tightly clustered at 7-8 (24 of 45), lowest std in sleep subcategory — most consistent listener experience. Qualitative (n=15) reveals unusual listener mix: 'A client/patient' (6) and 'Someone I support' (5) outnumber 'Myself' (4), suggesting high caregiver/clinician deployment. Longest settle in subcategory (10 min for 7) with 3 'Too busy' rhythm opinions. Semantic clustering tightly links to H-SL-L1 (sim=0.869).
by_protocol.H-SL-L3: Midnight holds meaningful confidence (n=28, mean=7.07, std=1.68, pct_pos=64%). Nearly identical to L2 in mean and distribution shape. Qualitative (n=22) richest for sleep: 'Noticeable' (7), 'Moderate' (5), 'Significant' (5) well-represented. Fastest settle in subcategory (5 min for 8), but 7 of 22 rated rhythm 'Slightly busy' — highest in sleep. Cross-run drift at 0.611 moderate but notable. AutoGluon does not specifically flag; selects H-SL-L1 as zone lead.
by_protocol.T-CF-L1: Ember shows meaningful confidence (n=34, mean=6.74, std=1.73, pct_pos=53%). Distribution peaks at 7-8 with noticeable left tail (9 scores at 6 or below). Qualitative (n=21): 'Slight improvement' modal (8), bimodal settle (immediate for 6, 10 min for 6), indicating two response populations. Positive music ('Liked it' 9, 'Loved it!' 6). Cross-run drift at 0.730, relatively stable. AutoGluon does not specifically flag.
by_protocol.T-CF-L2: Current demonstrates meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.79, std=1.41, pct_pos=58%). Nearly identical to L1 in outcome but tighter std (1.41 vs 1.73), suggesting more consistent experience. Qualitative (n=15): 'Significant improvement' modal (5), inverting L1's 'Slight' pattern — L2 intensity pushes HOLD effect from subtle to pronounced. Music opinion notably strong ('Loved it!' 6, 'Liked it' 6) with no negatives. Settle clusters at 10 min.
by_protocol.T-CF-L3: Aurora shows developing confidence (n=18, mean=7.28, std=1.32, pct_pos=67%) with lowest variance in Creative Flow. Distribution peaks at 7-8 with minimal tail. Qualitative (n=25) reports 'Noticeable' (7) and 'Significant' (6) improvements; settle at 5-10 min for most. Music opinion divided ('Liked it' 9, 'Neutral' 7) — potentially higher n needed. YouTube views=7. Drift at 0.602 suggests recent analytical shift.
by_protocol.T-FC-L1: Compass shows meaningful confidence (n=41, mean=6.46, std=1.82, pct_pos=56%) — lowest THRIVING protocol. Distribution spreads wide with mode at 6-7 but high-score tail (10 respondents at 9-10). Qualitative (n=23): 'Noticeable improvement' dominant; rapid settle for many; rhythm 'Slightly busy' (9 of 23) — flagged by two analyzers as potential issue for a Focus HOLD protocol. YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts 7.73 for focus zone but routes to L3, suggesting L1 underperforms zone potential.
by_protocol.T-FC-L2: Waypoint has meaningful confidence (n=32, mean=7.19, std=1.60, pct_pos=66%). Distribution tighter with clear mode at 7-8. Qualitative (n=17): 'Moderate' (5) and 'Significant' (5) gains; settle at 10 min dominant; balanced rhythm. YouTube views=4. AutoGluon prediction (7.73) modestly higher than observed; drift at 0.633 shows moderate stability.
by_protocol.T-FC-L3: Summit carries meaningful confidence (n=35, mean=7.63, std=1.97, pct_pos=71%) — highest-scoring protocol in entire library. Distribution is bimodal: cluster at 6-7 and another at 9-10 (10 respondents). Qualitative (n=29): highest 'Significant improvement' count (11); settle at 10 min for most; rhythm balanced or slightly busy. YouTube views=15 — largest reach in THRIVING. AutoGluon focus score 7.73 aligns well; cluster analysis shows analytical distinction (mean_sim=0.692).
by_protocol.T-MB-L1: Stone shows meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=7.04, std=1.34, pct_pos=64%). Distribution centered at 7-8 with low tail. Qualitative (n=23): 'Moderate' (6), 'Noticeable' (5), 'Significant' (5) — balanced improvement; settle often 20 min; rhythm balanced. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts maintenance-zone 7.69 (higher than observed). Drift at 0.578 notably high, indicating evolving interpretation.
by_protocol.T-MB-L2: Axis shows meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.68, std=2.03, pct_pos=59%) — highest variance in THRIVING. Distribution wide with secondary peak at 9-10. Qualitative (n=16): 'Noticeable' dominant; settle at 5-10 min; rhythm balanced but many 'Slightly busy' (7). YouTube views=4. AutoGluon predicts 7.69; observed substantially lower. Flagged by two analyzers as potential inconsistency in HOLD arc at L2 intensity.
by_protocol.T-MB-L3: Orbit has developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.93, std=1.73, pct_pos=71%). Distribution modestly left-skewed. Qualitative (n=19): 'Noticeable' improvements; settle at 5 min for most; rhythm balanced. YouTube views=3. AutoGluon predicts 7.69, modest gap from observed. Drift at 0.554 shows notable analytical shift.
by_protocol.X-MC-L1: Cavern shows meaningful confidence (n=24, mean=6.75, std=1.62, pct_pos=54%). Distribution modestly centered with slight tail to 8-9. Qualitative (n=25): 'Noticeable' (10) and 'Moderate' (8); settle ~10 min; rhythm balanced or very settling. Music opinion strong ('Loved it!' 9, 'Liked it' 9). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts mind-clarity 7.54 (higher than observed); cross-bucket links to X-ME-L1/L2.
by_protocol.X-MC-L2: Starlight has developing confidence (n=16, mean=6.81, std=1.64, pct_pos=56%). Distribution symmetric similar to L1. YouTube views=12 — highest in TRANSFORMING. Qualitative (n=20): 'Significant' (7), 'Moderate' (6); settle split between immediate and 10 min; rhythm 'Very settling' (12). AutoGluon predicts 7.54; cross-bucket pattern consistent with overlap with X-ME protocols.
by_protocol.X-MC-L3: Cosmos has developing confidence (n=10, mean=5.70, std=2.45, pct_pos=50%) — lowest mean, highest variance in library. Distribution shows scores at 3, 7, 9 suggesting possible population split. Qualitative (n=17): 'Noticeable' (9) dominant but more 'No effect' (5) than other X-MC. YouTube views=8. Two analyzers flagged this as candidate population mismatch requiring subgroup analysis before increasing n. AutoGluon prediction unavailable due to low n.
by_protocol.X-MD-L1: Footpath shows meaningful confidence (n=22, mean=6.36, std=2.01, pct_pos=45%) — lowest in Motivation subcategory. Distribution peaks at 5-6. Qualitative (n=17): 'Noticeable' (6), 'Significant' (5); settle at 5 min; music 'Liked it' (8) and 'Neutral' (5). YouTube views=5. AutoGluon predicts motivation-zone 7.876 but routes to X-MD-L1 as cross-bucket destination.
by_protocol.X-MD-L2: Trail carries developing confidence (n=14, mean=6.21, std=1.63, pct_pos=43%) with 20-min modal settle time. Distribution moderately centered. Qualitative (n=21): 'Noticeable' (6), 'Moderate' (5); 'Slightly busy' as second-common rhythm opinion (6). YouTube views=7. Flagged by one analyzer as potentially too gradual ASCENT curve requiring steeper phi-sigmoid slope.
by_protocol.X-MD-L3: Switchback has developing confidence (n=13, mean=7.15, std=2.38, pct_pos=62%) — highest in Motivation subcategory despite low n. Distribution shows high-score tail. Qualitative (n=17): 'Moderate' (8), 'Noticeable' (5); fast settle (5 min); music positive. YouTube views=7. Highest cross-run drift (sim=0.507) indicates substantially shifting interpretation across runs.
by_protocol.X-ME-L1: Breeze shows meaningful confidence (n=39, mean=6.74, std=1.94, pct_pos=54%). Distribution spreads across 4-10. Qualitative (n=22): 'Moderate' (7), 'Significant' (6); settle at 20 min; music 'Loved it!' (10), 'Neutral' (6). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon predicts X-ME-L1 as cross-bucket destination for nearly every zone — flagged as potential over-indexing on latent feature.
by_protocol.X-ME-L2: Sunrise carries meaningful confidence (n=25, mean=6.76, std=1.71, pct_pos=64%). Distribution more right-skewed than L1. Qualitative (n=15): 'Significant' (7), 'Moderate' (4); settle split 20 min and immediate; music positive ('Liked it' 7, 'Loved it!' 6). YouTube views=8. AutoGluon cross-bucket routing includes this as destination for multiple zones.
by_protocol.X-ME-L3: Updraft shows developing confidence (n=18, mean=6.28, std=1.74, pct_pos=56%) — inverts expected ASCENT progression (lower than L1 and L2). Distribution peaks at 7-8. Qualitative (n=25): 'Noticeable' (9), 'No effect' (5) — highest 'No effect' in series; settle at 5 min for most. Music opinion positive. YouTube views=4. Two analyzers flagged inverted level progression as structurally wrong for ASCENT — L3 may overshoot optimal arousal window.

by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L1: Meadow is the most responded-to protocol in the library, with listeners reporting good anxiety relief and a pleasant listening experience — though it hasn't reached many people on YouTube yet.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L2: Cottage performs well for anxiety but shows more mixed reactions than Meadow — some listeners settled quickly, others took longer, suggesting it works differently depending on the person.
by_protocol_plain.H-AX-L3: Valley shows strong results but takes longer to work than the other anxiety protocols — listeners who stick with it report good outcomes, but it requires more patience.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L1: Anchor is designed for crisis moments, so the scores are lower than other HEALING protocols — that's expected since people using it are in acute distress. But those who use it often report significant relief.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L2: Harbor performs slightly better than Anchor for crisis moments, with many listeners finding it very settling — though there's a split in how people feel about the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-CR-L3: Horizon works fastest for crisis — many people settle within the first minute — but it also has the most mixed reviews in the crisis category, with some not liking the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L1: Pebble is the most popular protocol in the library with the best outcomes for over-stimulation relief — listeners love the experience and recommend it for overwhelming moments.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L2: Rainfall works well too, though slightly less than Pebble — it takes about 10 minutes to settle, and some listeners have neutral feelings about the music.
by_protocol_plain.H-OS-L3: Glacier is interesting because it's actually faster and more effective than Rainfall despite being the strongest intensity — paradoxically, some listeners find the deeper level more calming.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L1: Lantern is the strongest sleep protocol with a solid track record — it helps people settle and creates good improvement reported across multiple listeners.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L2: Hammock performs similarly to Lantern with very consistent results — it's often used by caregivers or clinicians with their clients rather than for personal use.
by_protocol_plain.H-SL-L3: Midnight shows good sleep support with varied improvement levels — it's the fastest to work but some listeners find the rhythm slightly busy.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L1: Ember works for creative flow but the effect is subtle for many people — some feel it immediately, others need 10 minutes to settle into the experience.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L2: Current pushes past the subtle effect of Ember — more listeners report significant improvements, with strong positive feelings about the music.
by_protocol_plain.T-CF-L3: Aurora has the best creative flow results so far with positive ratings despite lower counts — seems to work well but needs more data to be sure.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L1: Compass is the weakest THRIVING protocol — some listeners get good results but overall it's underperforming, with many finding the rhythm slightly too busy for a focus protocol.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L2: Waypoint shows solid focus improvement — most listeners settle around 10 minutes and report meaningful gains in clarity.
by_protocol_plain.T-FC-L3: Summit is the highest-performing protocol in the entire library — strong improvement reports and the best YouTube reach in THRIVING, suggesting it genuinely works.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L1: Stone provides good maintenance and balance support — most listeners need about 20 minutes to fully settle but report positive outcomes.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L2: Axis has inconsistent results with high variance — some love it, others don't feel much effect, suggesting it might work best for specific listener types.
by_protocol_plain.T-MB-L3: Orbit shows good maintenance support with fast action — works quickly but has limited data so far.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L1: Cavern offers clear mind benefits with good music reception — listeners like it and report noticing improvements.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L2: Starlight is actually the most-watched TRANSFORMING video — strong 'very settling' feedback and significant improvement reports despite moderate scores.
by_protocol_plain.X-MC-L3: Cosmos has low scores and very mixed results — few people have tried it and the responses are all over the place, making it hard to know if it works.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L1: Footpath has the lowest motivation scores with wide variance — doesn't seem to consistently work, with some experiencing improvement but others not.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L2: Trail takes the longest to work in the motivation category — 20 minutes to settle suggests the effect may be too gradual for quick needs.
by_protocol_plain.X-MD-L3: Switchback actually performs best for motivation despite low sample numbers — suggests higher intensity may be needed to drive motivation.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L1: Breeze shows moderate mood improvement — more people need 20 minutes to settle, but those who stick with it report good results.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L2: Sunrise performs similarly to Breeze with strong significant improvement reports — listeners enjoy the music and experience positive effects.
by_protocol_plain.X-ME-L3: Updraft paradoxically scores lower than its predecessors despite being strongest — appears to overshoot the sweet spot for mood and energy.
